message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix J-004 in src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
What: _experiment_id re-fetches the experiment on a concurrent create race (RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS) instead of propagating and dropping the run.
Test(s): test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py:84:        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)

55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
69	        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
70	        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
71	        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
72	        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
73	        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
74	        try:
75	            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
76	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
77	            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
78	            if exp is not None:
79	                return str(exp.experiment_id)
80	            raise
81	
82	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
83	        client = self._client()
84	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
85	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
86	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
87	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
88	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
89	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
90	        try:
91	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
92	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
93	        except Exception:
94	            try:
95	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
96	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
97	                pass
98	            raise
99	        return run_id

content.text: The fix looks correct. Now let me read the test to verify it discriminates.

content.content:
126	def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
127	    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
128	    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
129	    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
130	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
131	
132	    class _Exp:
133	        experiment_id = "exp-7"
134	
135	    class RacingClient:
136	        def __init__(self):
137	            self.calls = 0
138	
139	        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
140	            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
141	            self.calls += 1
142	            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
143	
144	        def create_experiment(self, name):
145	            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
146	
147	    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
148	    client = RacingClient()
149	    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
150	
151	
152	def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
153	    # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
154	    # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
155	    # requiring the mlflow extra.
156	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
157	    events = []
158	
159	    class _RunInfo:
160	        run_id = "run-xyz"
161	
162	    class _Run:
163	        info = _RunInfo()
164	
165	    class _FakeClient:
166	        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
167	            return None
168	
169	        def create_experiment(self, name):
170	            return "exp-1"
171	
172	        def create_run(self, exp_id, run_name=None):
173	            events.append(("create", run_name))
174	            return _Run()
175	
176	        def log_param(self, run_id, k, v):
177	            raise RuntimeError("store unreachable")
178	
179	        def set_terminated(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
180	            events.append(("terminated", run_id, status))
181	
182	    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
183	    monkeypatch.setattr(t, "_client", lambda: _FakeClient())
184	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="store unreachable"):
185	        t.start_run("study", {"k": "v"})
186	    assert ("terminated", "run-xyz", "FAILED") in events  # orphan run was not left RUNNING
187	
188	
189	def test_unconfigured_tracer_fails_loud():
190	    with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError):
191	        with UnconfiguredTracer().span("x"):
192	            pass
193	
194

            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))

content.text: Test discriminates: passes with fix, fails when reverted. File restored. Let me verify restoration.

File restored (no diff). 

Fix is appropriate and complete:

**Appropriate:** `_experiment_id` now wraps `create_experiment` in try/except. On the concurrent-create race, instead of propagating the exception (which would bubble out of `start_run`, leaving `run_id=None` and silently dropping all metrics for the study), it re-fetches the now-existing experiment via `get_experiment_by_name` and returns its id. If the re-fetch still yields `None` (genuine failure, not a race), it re-raises — so non-race errors are not swallowed. Idiomatic with explicit `# noqa: BLE001` justification matching the codebase's existing style. No regression: the happy path (existing experiment, or clean create) is unchanged.

**Complete / discriminates:** `test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race` uses a `RacingClient` whose first `get_experiment_by_name` returns `None`, whose `create_experiment` raises `RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS`, and whose second `get_experiment_by_name` returns the winner's experiment. It asserts `_experiment_id` returns `"exp-7"`. With the fix: passes. With the except-branch reverted to a bare `return str(client.create_experiment(...))`: fails with the RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS RuntimeError. Confirmed by running both states.

input.reasoning:
Read the changed file /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py (lines 64-80) and the test at tests/test_observability.py:126.

APPROPRIATE: _experiment_id wraps client.create_experiment in try/except. On the concurrent-create race (RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS), instead of propagating — which would bubble out of start_run leaving run_id=None and silently dropping all metrics + provenance for the study — it re-fetches via get_experiment_by_name and returns the winner's experiment_id. Crucially, if the re-fetch still returns None (a genuine non-race failure), it re-raises, so real errors are not swallowed. Happy paths (existing experiment, clean create) are unchanged; no regression. Style matches codebase (explicit noqa: BLE001 with justification).

COMPLETE / DISCRIMINATES: Verified by execution with .venv/bin/python (3.14; system python3 too old for slots dataclass). Test passes as-is (1 passed). I reverted the except-branch to a bare `return str(client.create_experiment(...))` and re-ran: the test FAILED with RuntimeError RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS at tracking line. Restored the file (git diff clean). The test drives _experiment_id directly with a RacingClient (first get_experiment_by_name=None, create_experiment raises RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS, second get_experiment_by_name returns _Exp(experiment_id='exp-7')) and asserts the result is 'exp-7' — exactly the re-fetch path. Confirmed it fails if reverted.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. The J-004 fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py` is both appropriate and complete, and the test `test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race` discriminates (passes with the fix, fails when reverted).
